title: Gettysburg full three-day order-of-battle register (audit working grain)
status: PROPOSAL — audit-research-1 branch; nothing canonical until the owner rules
grain: brigade + battery/battalion base grain; regiments enumerated as name-only strings inside brigade entries (per the unit-truth-spec scope ruling)

conventions.castStatus: in-build unit id from app/Assets/Battle/gettysburg-july3.json where a register entry matches a build unit; else 'not-yet-cast'. In-build child regiments (e.g. csa-8va, us-13vt, us-8oh) are represented inside their brigade entry's regiments list, not as separate register entries.
conventions.strengthFigures: only where docs/research/2026-06-13-oob-strengths.md or the 2026-07-02 full-cast survey carries a figure; disagreements preserved verbatim, never averaged (OOB research doctrine).
conventions.daysPresent: calendar days (July N) the body was on or immediately adjacent to the field; [] = never on the field (e.g. Huey at Westminster). Off-square July 3 positions (East Cavalry Field, Fairfield, Westminster, Cashtown) are flagged in notes/arrivalNote per the full-cast survey's OFF-MAP ruling.
conventions.commander: commander at Gettysburg with known mid-battle successions (k/w/mw/c annotations per the OR-derived record).
conventions.sources: ids resolve in reconstruction/sources/sources.json. Structure and successions: or-27-oob + wikipedia-oob-union/-csa (verified 2026-07-10); activity notes: stone-sentinels War Department tablets via the 2026-07-02 full-cast survey.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 1st Division, I Corps (Iron Brigade)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Solomon Meredith (w July 1); Col. William W. Robinson
entries.notes: Wrecked July 1 in McPherson's/Herbst Woods; July 2-3 Culp's Hill trenches beside XII Corps (tablet).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 1st Division, I Corps
entries.arrivalNote: 7th Indiana (train guard July 1) rejoined evening July 1.
entries.notes: Railroad-cut fight July 1; Culp's Hill July 2-3.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 2nd Division, I Corps (Paul's)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Gabriel R. Paul (w July 1); Col. Samuel H. Leonard (w); Col. Adrian R. Root (w&c); Col. Richard Coulter (w); Col. Peter Lyle
entries.notes: Five successive commanders July 1 (Oak Ridge). July 3: moved to right of II Corps at 3 P.M. (division tablet).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 2nd Division, I Corps
entries.notes: Destroyed Iverson's brigade July 1 (Oak Ridge). July 3 mover with Robinson's division (3 P.M., tablet).

entries.name: 3rd Division, I Corps headquarters (division command)
entries.commander: Maj. Gen. Abner Doubleday (corps command July 1 after Reynolds fell; division command July 2-3 after Newton superseded him)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-4 flag): late litigious report; his own July 3 station open; late-stated clock cluster member (-53). Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-i-3-hq-doubleday.md.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 3rd Division, I Corps (Biddle's)
entries.commander: Col. Chapman Biddle (July 1); Brig. Gen. Thomas A. Rowley; Col. Theodore B. Gates at the line July 3
entries.notes: July 3 on Cemetery Ridge left of Gibbon; 'assisted in repelling Longstreet's assault' (tablet).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 3rd Division, I Corps (Stone's)
entries.commander: Col. Roy Stone (w July 1); Col. Langhorne Wister (w July 1); Col. Edmund L. Dana
entries.notes: Bucktail brigade; McPherson farm July 1; Cemetery Ridge July 3.

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 3rd Division, I Corps (Stannard's 2nd Vermont Brigade)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. George J. Stannard (w July 3); Col. Francis V. Randall
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived evening July 1 (nine-months men, joined the army mid-campaign).
entries.notes: 12th and 15th VT on train guard; 13th/14th/16th made the July 3 flank attack (ED corpus, vt13-monument).

strengthFigures.note: Sum of the three engaged regiments (13th VT 480 + 14th VT 647 + 16th VT 661 = 1,788) does not match — ~160-man gap flagged, not reconciled.

entries.name: 2nd Maine Battery (B)
entries.notes: July 1 Chambersburg Pike; July 3 relieved by Edgell, sent to the rear — 'no action July 3' (documented negative, full-cast survey).

entries.name: 5th Maine Battery (E)
entries.commander: Capt. Greenlief T. Stevens (w July 2); Lt. Edward N. Whittier
entries.notes: Stevens Knoll between Cemetery and Culp's Hills July 2-3.

entries.name: Battery L (with E), 1st New York Light
entries.commander: Capt. Gilbert H. Reynolds (w July 1); Lt. George Breck

entries.name: Battery B, 1st Pennsylvania Light
entries.notes: July 3: East Cemetery Hill to Hancock Ave at 3 P.M. 'during a heavy cannonade' — documented in-window battery track (monument).

entries.name: Battery B, 4th US
entries.notes: Astride the Baltimore Pike, East Cemetery Hill July 2-3.

entries.name: II Corps headquarters (corps command)
entries.commander: Maj. Gen. Winfield S. Hancock (w July 3); Brig. Gen. John C. Caldwell (per seniority after the wounding; Hancock retained direction until carried off)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-3/4 flag): command record for the corps that received the July 3 assault; Hancock's wounding is a CONFLICT record by design (Vermont during-the-maneuver reading vs his own end-of-action framing). Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-ii-hq-hancock.md.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 1st Division, II Corps (Cross's)
entries.commander: Col. Edward E. Cross (mw July 2); Col. H. Boyd McKeen
entries.arrivalNote: II Corps on the field early July 2 (advance elements evening July 1).
entries.notes: Wheatfield July 2; July 3 behind breastworks south end of the II Corps front (tablet).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 1st Division, II Corps (Irish Brigade)
entries.notes: Wheatfield July 2.

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 1st Division, II Corps (Zook's)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Samuel K. Zook (mw July 2); Lt. Col. John Fraser
entries.notes: Wheatfield July 2.

entries.name: 4th Brigade, 1st Division, II Corps
entries.notes: Deepest Wheatfield penetration July 2 (Rose farm).

entries.name: 2nd Division, II Corps headquarters (division command)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. John Gibbon (w July 3); Brig. Gen. William Harrow
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-4 flag): the first wounding pin of the July 3 climax pair (Hancock's report sequences Gibbon w then his own, both 'toward the close'). Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-ii-2-hq-gibbon.md.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 2nd Division, II Corps (Harrow's)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. William Harrow; Col. Francis E. Heath
entries.notes: 1st Minnesota's July 2 charge; July 3 repulse at the copse (in-build).

strengthFigures.note: 19th ME 405 'effective July 2'; 15th MA 304; 82nd NY 394; 1st MN three figures at three scopes: 420 regiment / 262 (8 cos, July 2 charge) / 330 'engaged'. Casualties: 768 (WD/NPS marker) vs 722 (Harrow's own report).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 2nd Division, II Corps (Philadelphia Brigade)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Alexander S. Webb (w July 3)
entries.notes: The Angle (in-build, Angle cast).

strengthFigures.note: Per-regiment: 69th PA 258; 71st PA 331; 72nd PA 458 'present at Gettysburg' vs 473 'present for duty'; 106th PA 335 (23 off + 312 men) vs 312 men (two accounts, same page).

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 2nd Division, II Corps (Hall's)
entries.notes: In-build, Angle cast.

strengthFigures.note: Per-regiment: 19th MA 231; 7th MI 165; 42nd NY 197; 20th MA and 59th NY not located.

entries.name: 3rd Division, II Corps headquarters (division command)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-4 flag): the four-lines repulse record on the Pettigrew front; the famous flag-drag ride is absent from his report and is not asserted. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-ii-3-hq-hays.md.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 3rd Division, II Corps (Carroll's)
entries.notes: Counterattacked East Cemetery Hill night July 2; July 3 on East Cemetery Hill (in-build minus the 8th Ohio, which is a separate in-build unit us-8oh).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 3rd Division, II Corps (Smyth's)
entries.commander: Col. Thomas A. Smyth (w July 3); Lt. Col. Francis E. Pierce
entries.notes: Bliss farm fights July 2-3; in-build.

strengthFigures.note: 14th CT 160 in action; 1st DE 288; 12th NJ 532; 10th NY Bn and 108th NY not located.

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 3rd Division, II Corps (Willard's/Sherrill's)
entries.commander: Col. George L. Willard (k July 2); Col. Eliakim Sherrill (mw July 3); Lt. Col. James M. Bull
entries.notes: Counterattacked Barksdale July 2; in-build.

strengthFigures.note: 111th NY 390 engaged (8 cos); 125th NY 500 (monument); 126th NY 455; 39th NY (4 cos) not located.

strengthFigures.figure: 600 men (brigade aggregate, not broken out by battery)

entries.name: Battery B, 1st New York Light (14th NY Battery attached)
entries.commander: Capt. James McKay Rorty (k July 3); Lt. Albert S. Sheldon (w July 3); Lt. Robert E. Rogers

entries.name: Battery B, 1st Rhode Island Light
entries.commander: Lt. T. Fred Brown (w July 2); Lt. William S. Perrin

entries.name: Battery I, 1st US
entries.commander: Lt. George A. Woodruff (mw July 3); Lt. Tully McCrea
entries.notes: Ziegler's Grove.

strengthFigures.note: Casualty readings conflict between fetches: 30 total vs 25 total — both kept.

entries.name: Battery A, 4th US
entries.commander: Lt. Alonzo H. Cushing (k July 3); Sgt. Frederick Fuger
entries.notes: The Angle (Angle cast).

strengthFigures.source: U.S. Army Medal of Honor page (army-moh-cushing)

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 1st Division, III Corps (Graham's)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Charles K. Graham (w&c July 2); Col. Andrew H. Tippin; Col. Henry J. Madill at the line July 3
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived evening July 1 via the Emmitsburg Road.
entries.notes: Peach Orchard salient July 2; July 3 in army reserve behind the left-centre (tablet).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 1st Division, III Corps (Ward's)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. J. H. Hobart Ward; Col. Hiram Berdan
entries.notes: Devil's Den/Houck's Ridge July 2.

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 1st Division, III Corps
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived ~10 A.M. July 2 (Emmitsburg train escort).
entries.notes: Wheatfield July 2.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 2nd Division, III Corps
entries.notes: Emmitsburg Road line July 2.

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 2nd Division, III Corps
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived ~10 A.M. July 2 with de Trobriand (Emmitsburg column).
entries.notes: Broken up as reinforcement packets July 2.

entries.name: 2nd Division, III Corps headquarters (division command)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Andrew A. Humphreys (unwounded; two horses shot under him July 2 per his report class)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 7 (register maintenance): command record for the division whose July 2 fighting withdrawal is the CA-J2A-9/10 physics spine (5,000 into action / 2,088 lost, Humphreys's own report; the Birney oblique-line order chain; Sickles's wounding pin rides this record via Birney). Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-iii-2-hq-humphreys.md.

entries.name: Artillery Brigade, III Corps
entries.commander: Capt. George E. Randolph (w July 2); Capt. A. Judson Clark

entries.commander: Capt. A. Judson Clark; Lt. Robert Sims

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 1st Division, V Corps

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 3rd Division, V Corps (Pennsylvania Reserves)

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 3rd Division, V Corps (Pennsylvania Reserves)

entries.notes: Little Round Top north slope July 2.

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 1st Division, VI Corps

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 3rd Division, VI Corps

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 3rd Division, VI Corps
entries.notes: 'Not engaged but subject to artillery fire' (tablet); 69 casualties.

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 3rd Division, VI Corps (Wheaton's)

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 1st Division, XI Corps

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 2nd Division, XI Corps

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 3rd Division, XI Corps (Schimmelfennig's)

entries.name: Battery I, 1st New York Light
entries.notes: East Cemetery Hill; overrun and retaken July 2.

entries.name: 13th New York Independent Battery
entries.notes: OR report transcribed (13nybattery.com): reserve during the cannonade, to the II Corps line ~4 P.M. July 3.

entries.name: Battery K, 1st Ohio Light
entries.notes: Wrecked July 1; July 3 role unattested (flag).

entries.name: Battery G, 4th US
entries.commander: Lt. Bayard Wilkeson (mw July 1); Lt. Eugene A. Bancroft

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 1st Division, XII Corps
entries.arrivalNote: XII Corps arrived ~5 P.M. July 1 (Two Taverns).
entries.notes: Culp's Hill lower works.

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 1st Division, XII Corps (Lockwood's)
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived midday July 2 (unbrigaded reinforcement, attached to Williams).

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 1st Division, XII Corps (Ruger's/Colgrove's)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Thomas H. Ruger; Col. Silas Colgrove
entries.notes: Spangler's Spring/McAllister's Woods; the 2nd MA-27th IN charge July 3 morning.

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 2nd Division, XII Corps (Kane's/Cobham's)
entries.commander: Col. George A. Cobham Jr.; Brig. Gen. Thomas L. Kane

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 2nd Division, XII Corps (Greene's)
entries.notes: Held the Culp's Hill works alone night July 2; the seven-hour July 3 morning fight.

entries.name: Battery M, 1st New York Light
entries.notes: McAllister's Hill (Baltimore Pike/Rock Creek).

entries.name: Independent Battery E, Pennsylvania Light (Knap's)
entries.notes: Powers Hill.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 1st Division, Cavalry Corps (Gamble's)
entries.arrivalNote: Buford's July 1 delaying fight; withdrew toward Taneytown/Westminster July 2 — not on the field July 3.

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 1st Division, Cavalry Corps (Devin's)
entries.arrivalNote: With Gamble July 1; withdrew July 2 — not on the field July 3.

entries.name: Reserve Brigade, 1st Division, Cavalry Corps (Merritt's)
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived from Emmitsburg July 3 (arrival stated 11:00/13:00/15:00 across sources — disagreement encoded in the full-cast survey).
entries.notes: South Cavalry Field, astride the Emmitsburg Road at the map's south edge.

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 2nd Division, Cavalry Corps (McIntosh's)
entries.arrivalNote: Brinkerhoff's Ridge July 2 evening; East Cavalry Field July 3 (off the terrain square).

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 2nd Division, Cavalry Corps (Huey's)
entries.arrivalNote: Detached at Westminster guarding trains — never on the field.

entries.name: 3rd Brigade, 2nd Division, Cavalry Corps (J. Irvin Gregg's)
entries.arrivalNote: Hanover Road sector July 2-3; East Cavalry Field fringe July 3 (off the terrain square).

entries.name: 1st Brigade, 3rd Division, Cavalry Corps (Farnsworth's)
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Elon J. Farnsworth (k July 3); Col. Nathaniel P. Richmond
entries.arrivalNote: Hunterstown July 2 (off-map); arrived South Cavalry Field ~13:00 July 3.
entries.notes: Farnsworth's charge ~17:00+ July 3 (post-window for the current slice).

strengthFigures.source: NPS Gettysburg Seminar 9 essay 8 (Snell), via full-cast survey

entries.name: 2nd Brigade, 3rd Division, Cavalry Corps (Custer's)
entries.arrivalNote: Hunterstown July 2; East Cavalry Field July 3 (off the terrain square).

entries.name: 9th Michigan Battery
entries.notes: East Cavalry Field July 3 per the OOB; Bachelder sheet 8 labels a 'Daniels' on McGilvery's line — sheet-vs-OOB conflict, encoded in the full-cast survey.

entries.name: Battery M, 2nd US
entries.commander: Lt. A. C. M. Pennington Jr.
entries.notes: East Cavalry Field July 3 (off-map).

entries.name: Battery E, 4th US
entries.notes: South Cavalry Field July 3; shelled Law's line in-window (in-build).

entries.name: Batteries E & G, 1st US

entries.name: Battery K, 1st US

entries.name: Battery A, 2nd US
entries.notes: With Gamble July 1 (opening fight).

entries.name: Battery C, 3rd US
entries.notes: With Huey at Westminster — never on the field.

entries.name: Artillery Reserve headquarters, park and ammunition train
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Robert O. Tyler; Capt. James M. Robertson (after Tyler's July 3 incapacitation)
entries.notes: 2,375 men, 114 guns nominal (full-cast survey); park displaced south behind Little Round Top under the July 3 overshoots (Hunt). Train guard: 4th New Jersey (7 cos).

entries.name: Artillery command, Army of the Potomac (Chief of Artillery)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 1 (anchor-executor layer): Hunt's July 3 ammunition-conservation order IS the executor of chain anchors CA-J3A-4/5 and needed a command row to carry its dossier; previously the register had no army-level artillery command entry. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-arty-hq-hunt.md.

entries.name: Battery H, 1st US
entries.commander: Lt. Chandler P. Eakin (w July 2); Lt. Philip D. Mason

entries.name: 5th Massachusetts Battery (E) (10th NY Battery attached)
entries.notes: The best single firing attestation on McGilvery's July 3 line (monument text, full-cast survey).

entries.name: 9th Massachusetts Battery
entries.commander: Capt. John Bigelow (w July 2); Lt. Richard S. Milton
entries.notes: Trostle farm stand July 2; remnant on McGilvery's line July 3.

entries.name: Battery B, 1st Connecticut Heavy
entries.notes: Present; 'not engaged' (full-cast survey).

entries.name: Battery M, 1st Connecticut Heavy
entries.notes: Present; 'not engaged' (full-cast survey).

entries.name: 5th New York Independent Battery
entries.notes: 20-pdr Parrotts on Cemetery Hill.

entries.name: Batteries F & G, 1st Pennsylvania Light
entries.notes: East Cemetery Hill; overrun and retaken night July 2.

entries.name: Battery A, 1st Maryland Light
entries.notes: Powers Hill July 2-3.

entries.name: Battery K, 1st New York Light (11th NY Battery attached)
Provost Guard and army headquarters detachments (Brig. Gen. Marsena R. Patrick)
entries.notes: 93rd New York, 8th US (8 cos), 2nd Pennsylvania Cavalry, 6th Pennsylvania Cavalry Cos. E & I, detachments of 1st/6th US Cavalry; 93rd NY and 8th US 'stationed at Taneytown and not engaged' (Stone Sentinels). The Engineer Brigade (Benham) left for Washington July 1 and was NOT at Gettysburg — do not model.

entries.name: Kershaw's Brigade, McLaws's Division
entries.arrivalNote: McLaws's division on the field morning July 2 (Marsh Creek night July 1).
entries.notes: Stony Hill/Rose farm July 2; July 3 Peach Orchard line, extended right toward Hood's left ~13:00 (tablet, full-cast survey).

entries.name: Semmes's Brigade, McLaws's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Paul J. Semmes (mw July 2); Col. Goode Bryan

entries.name: Barksdale's Brigade, McLaws's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. William Barksdale (mw July 2); Col. Benjamin G. Humphreys
entries.notes: Smashed the Peach Orchard salient July 2.

entries.name: Pickett's Division headquarters (division command)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-3 flag): division command record for the assault column's right wing; the suppressed report (Lee's return-for-destruction order, or-27-3-lee-pickett) is the record's defining negative. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/csa-1c-pic-hq-pickett.md.

entries.name: Garnett's Brigade, Pickett's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Richard B. Garnett (k July 3); Maj. Charles S. Peyton
entries.arrivalNote: Division reached the field late afternoon/evening July 2 (guarding trains at Chambersburg until July 2); engaged July 3 only.
entries.notes: Angle cast (V2 corpus).

entries.name: Kemper's Brigade, Pickett's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. James L. Kemper (w&c July 3, recovered by CSA); Col. Joseph Mayo Jr.
entries.arrivalNote: With the division (see Garnett entry).
entries.notes: Angle cast.

entries.name: Armistead's Brigade, Pickett's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Lewis A. Armistead (mw&c July 3, d. July 5); Col. William R. Aylett
entries.arrivalNote: With the division (see Garnett entry).
entries.notes: Angle cast (ED-2, ED-8).

strengthFigures.source: Col. W. R. Aylett report, July 12 1863, via Encyclopedia Virginia
strengthFigures.note: Casualty cross-check, not a strength figure. A '1,950 strength' surfaced only in a search summary — flagged unreliable, do not cite.

entries.name: Law's Brigade, Hood's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Evander M. Law (to division July 2); Col. James L. Sheffield
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived ~noon July 2 after a ~24-28 mile march from New Guilford.
entries.notes: Round Tops July 2; west slope Big Round Top July 3.

entries.name: Robertson's Texas Brigade, Hood's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Jerome B. Robertson (w July 2; command July 3 disputed — Robertson vs Lt. Col. P. A. Work, flag per full-cast survey)

entries.name: Anderson's Georgia Brigade, Hood's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. George T. Anderson (w July 2); Lt. Col. William Luffman
entries.notes: Rose farm July 3; elements shifted south vs Merritt ~15:00+ (full-cast survey).

entries.name: Benning's Brigade, Hood's Division
entries.notes: Houck's Ridge July 3; the 15th GA fight is ~17:00+ July 3 (post-window).

entries.name: Cabell's Artillery Battalion (McLaws's Division)
entries.notes: 16 guns; Emmitsburg Rd at Peach Orchard/Wheatfield Rd July 3; fired in the cannonade (tablet grade A, full-cast survey).

entries.name: Rowan (North Carolina) Artillery
entries.notes: Swung south vs Merritt ~15:00+ July 3 (in-build).

entries.name: First Corps artillery command (Chief of Artillery, Longstreet's corps)
entries.commander: Col. James B. Walton (nominal chief); Col. E. Porter Alexander (directing the corps artillery July 3 by Longstreet's order)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 1: the CA-J3A-1 signal order ran Longstreet -> Walton -> Miller's 3rd Co.; the Walton/Alexander divided-command arrangement is first-class context for the order chain and is carried in the Eshleman and Miller dossiers (reconstruction/dossiers/).

entries.name: Alexander's Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)
entries.commander: Col. E. Porter Alexander (directing the corps artillery July 3); Maj. Frank Huger at the battalion
entries.notes: ~24-26 guns; Peach Orchard -> NE corner Spangler's Woods; core of the July 3 cannonade; Alexander's notes to Pickett (grade A).

entries.name: Ashland (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Alexander's Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)
entries.commander: Capt. Pichegru Woolfolk Jr. (w July 2); Lt. James Woolfolk

entries.name: Bedford (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Alexander's Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Brooks (South Carolina) Artillery
parentChain: Alexander's Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Madison (Louisiana) Light Artillery
parentChain: Alexander's Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Virginia (Richmond) Battery
parentChain: Alexander's Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Virginia (Bath) Battery
parentChain: Alexander's Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Washington Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)
entries.notes: 10 guns; Peach Orchard July 3; fired the 13:07 signal guns (Miller's 3rd Company; tablet, full-cast survey).

entries.name: 1st Company, Washington Artillery
parentChain: Washington Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: 2nd Company, Washington Artillery
parentChain: Washington Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: 3rd Company, Washington Artillery
parentChain: Washington Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)
entries.notes: Fired the July 3 signal guns (tablet).

entries.name: 4th Company, Washington Artillery
parentChain: Washington Artillery Battalion (First Corps reserve artillery)
entries.commander: Capt. Joe Norcom (w July 3); Lt. H. A. Battles

entries.name: Hays's Brigade, Early's Division
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived via the Harrisburg Road ~mid-afternoon July 1.
entries.notes: Barlow Knoll July 1; stormed East Cemetery Hill night July 2; southern town streets July 3.

entries.name: Smith's Brigade, Early's Division
entries.notes: York Pike flank guard July 1-2; attached to Johnson July 3 (east of Rock Creek).

entries.name: Hoke's Brigade, Early's Division
entries.commander: Col. Isaac E. Avery (mw July 2); Col. Archibald C. Godwin
entries.notes: East Cemetery Hill assault July 2 (Avery's note: 'I died with my face to the enemy').

entries.name: Gordon's Brigade, Early's Division
entries.notes: Broke Barlow July 1; Winebrenner's Run July 3.

entries.name: Steuart's Brigade, Johnson's Division
entries.arrivalNote: Division arrived near dusk July 1 (did not fight July 1).
entries.notes: Culp's Hill lower works July 2-3; the July 3 morning fight; retired east of Rock Creek 10:30 A.M. (tablet).

entries.name: Stonewall Brigade, Johnson's Division
entries.notes: Brinkerhoff's Ridge July 2 evening (vs Gregg); Culp's Hill July 3 morning.

entries.name: Nicholls's Brigade, Johnson's Division
entries.commander: Col. Jesse M. Williams (Brig. Gen. Francis Nicholls absent, wounded earlier)

entries.name: Jones's Brigade, Johnson's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. John M. Jones (w July 2 evening, Culp's Hill first assault); Lt. Col. Robert H. Dungan
entries.notes: PASS-8 CORRECTION: Jones was wounded on the JULY 2 EVENING assault ('When near the first line of intrenchments... I received a flesh wound through the thigh', or-27-2-jones-jm p. 532; Johnson p. 504 concurs), not July 3 as previously carried.

entries.name: Daniel's Brigade, Rodes's Division
entries.notes: Railroad cuts fight July 1; attached to Johnson at Culp's Hill July 3 morning; east of Rock Creek afternoon (tablet).

entries.name: Iverson's Brigade, Rodes's Division
entries.notes: Nearly annihilated at Forney field July 1 (820 of 1,470, tablet); Long Lane July 2-3.

entries.name: Doles's Brigade, Rodes's Division
entries.notes: Long Lane July 2-3.

entries.name: Ramseur's Brigade, Rodes's Division
entries.notes: Long Lane July 2-3.

entries.name: O'Neal's Brigade, Rodes's Division
entries.notes: Repulsed at Oak Ridge July 1; attached to Johnson July 3 morning; east of Rock Creek afternoon (tablet).

entries.name: Jones's Artillery Battalion (Early's Division)
entries.notes: 16 guns; north of town July 3 — 'Not actively engaged' (tablet, documented negative).

entries.name: Andrews's Artillery Battalion (Johnson's Division; Maj. Latimer commanding)
entries.commander: Maj. Joseph W. Latimer (mw July 2); Capt. Charles I. Raine
entries.notes: 16 guns; the Benner's Hill duel July 2 (Latimer mortally wounded); July 3 20-pdr section ~1/2 mi N of Benner's Hill fired in the cannonade, rest in reserve (A-).

entries.name: 1st Maryland Battery
parentChain: Andrews's Artillery Battalion (Johnson's Division; Maj. Latimer commanding)

entries.name: Alleghany (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Andrews's Artillery Battalion (Johnson's Division; Maj. Latimer commanding)

entries.name: Chesapeake (Maryland) Artillery
parentChain: Andrews's Artillery Battalion (Johnson's Division; Maj. Latimer commanding)

entries.name: Lee (Virginia) Battery
parentChain: Andrews's Artillery Battalion (Johnson's Division; Maj. Latimer commanding)
entries.commander: Capt. Charles I. Raine; Lt. William W. Hardwicke

entries.name: Carter's Artillery Battalion (Rodes's Division)
entries.notes: 16 guns; Oak Hill July 1; July 3 Napoleons on Oak Hill largely silent while the rifles moved to Seminary Ridge near the railroad cut and 'took part in the great cannonade' (tablet, documented split).

entries.name: Morris (Virginia) Artillery
entries.commander: Capt. Richard C. M. Page (w July 1)

entries.name: Second Corps artillery command (Chief of Artillery, Ewell's corps)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-2/3/4 double flag): carries the four-class participation breakdown (ED-45) and the corps artillery command record; command position unstated in the report. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/csa-2c-arty-hq-brown.md.

entries.name: Dance's 1st Virginia Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery, Col. J. Thompson Brown)
entries.notes: 20 guns; Seminary Ridge near the Seminary/railroad cut July 3; Griffin's battery 'took part in the cannonade' (tablet).

entries.name: 2nd Richmond Howitzers
parentChain: Dance's 1st Virginia Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery, Col. J. Thompson Brown)

entries.name: 3rd Richmond Howitzers
parentChain: Dance's 1st Virginia Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery, Col. J. Thompson Brown)

entries.name: Powhatan (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Dance's 1st Virginia Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery, Col. J. Thompson Brown)

entries.name: Rockbridge (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Dance's 1st Virginia Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery, Col. J. Thompson Brown)

entries.name: Salem (Virginia) Flying Artillery
parentChain: Dance's 1st Virginia Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery, Col. J. Thompson Brown)
entries.notes: The battalion's attested July 3 cannonade participant (tablet).

entries.name: Nelson's Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery)
entries.notes: 11 guns; Benner's Hill area July 3 — 'opened about 12 M., firing 20 to 25 rounds' then silent (tablet, A-).

entries.name: Amherst (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Nelson's Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Fluvanna (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Nelson's Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Georgia (Milledge's) Battery
parentChain: Nelson's Artillery Battalion (Second Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Wilcox's Brigade, Anderson's Division
entries.arrivalNote: Division arrived afternoon July 1 (not engaged July 1); in line July 2.
entries.notes: July 2 assault to the Plum Run line; July 3 right-echelon support of Pickett, advanced and repulsed ~15:30-16:00.

strengthFigures.source: Stone Sentinels (wilcoxs-brigade), direct fetch of monument inscription
WebSearch summary of the SAME page (extraction conflict, kept not resolved)

entries.name: Mahone's Brigade, Anderson's Division
entries.notes: Trench line behind the crest, McMillan Woods sector July 3 (static, tablet).

entries.name: Wright's Brigade, Anderson's Division
entries.notes: Reached Cemetery Ridge July 2 (his contested claim); July 3 close support, brief advance ~15:30-16:00, recalled (full-cast survey).

entries.name: Perry's Brigade (Lang's Florida Brigade), Anderson's Division
entries.commander: Col. David Lang (Brig. Gen. Edward A. Perry absent, typhoid)
entries.notes: July 3 advanced on Wilcox's left in the post-charge supporting move; smashed by Phillips's battery and the 16th VT.

entries.name: Posey's Brigade, Anderson's Division
entries.notes: Bliss farm skirmishing July 2-3; trench line July 3 (static, tablet).

entries.name: Heth's Division headquarters (division command)
entries.commander: Maj. Gen. Henry Heth (w July 1); Brig. Gen. J. Johnston Pettigrew (July 2-3; w July 3)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-3 flag): left-wing division command record for the July 3 column (Pettigrew commanding). Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/csa-3c-het-hq-pettigrew.md.

entries.name: Pettigrew's Brigade (Marshall's), Heth's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. J. Johnston Pettigrew (to division July 1); Col. James K. Marshall (k July 3); Maj. John T. Jones
entries.notes: Herbst Woods July 1 (26th NC vs the Iron Brigade); assault column July 3.

strengthFigures.note: July-1 scope, not July-3 assault strength (systemic caveat: heavy July 1 losses precede the charge).

entries.name: Brockenbrough's Brigade, Heth's Division
entries.notes: Left of the July 3 assault line; broke early under the 8th Ohio and Osborn's guns.

strengthFigures.source: Col. R. M. Mayo official report, Aug. 13 1863, via NPS Seminar 12 essay 5 (secondary hop)
Bradley M. Gottfried, Gettysburg Magazine 23 (essay author flags as likely overestimate)

strengthFigures.figure: UNUSABLE: Stone Sentinels page returned figures byte-identical to Davis's brigade
strengthFigures.note: Confirmed duplication artifact on two fetches — do not encode.

entries.name: Archer's Brigade (Fry's), Heth's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. James J. Archer (c July 1); Col. Birkett D. Fry (w&c July 3); Lt. Col. Samuel G. Shepard
entries.notes: First infantry engaged July 1 (Archer captured); the guide brigade of the July 3 assault (fry-shsp7-1879).

strengthFigures.note: Same page narrative: 'less than 400 men remained at the end of July 3rd.' A '~1,200 (Busey & Martin)' figure surfaced only in an unverified search snippet — not citable.

entries.name: Davis's Brigade, Heth's Division
entries.arrivalNote: 11th Mississippi on train guard July 1 (rejoined for July 3).
entries.notes: Railroad cut July 1; assault column July 3.

strengthFigures.note: July-1 scope caveat as for Marshall's.

entries.name: Pender's Division headquarters (division command)
entries.commander: Maj. Gen. William D. Pender (mw July 2); Brig. Gen. James H. Lane (July 2 evening); Maj. Gen. Isaac R. Trimble (July 3, w+c); Lane resumed
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 5 (register maintenance, pass-3 flag): the Pender->Lane->Trimble->Lane succession chain; Trimble's third-line command record and his Emmitsburg-road-fence wounding conflict. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/csa-3c-pen-hq-trimble.md.

entries.name: Perrin's Brigade (McGowan's), Pender's Division
entries.commander: Col. Abner Perrin (Brig. Gen. Samuel McGowan absent, wounded)
entries.notes: Broke the Seminary Ridge line July 1; Long Lane July 2-3 — 'constantly engaged in skirmishing' (tablet, in-window emitter).

entries.name: Lane's Brigade, Pender's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. James H. Lane (to division July 2-3 intervals)
entries.notes: Trimble's second line, July 3 assault.

strengthFigures.source: Stone Sentinels (lanes-brigade), direct fetch quoting monument inscription
WebSearch summary of the SAME Stone Sentinels page (extraction conflict, kept not resolved)
strengthFigures.note: Same-page narrative: Lane's + Lowrance's combined 'could field no more than 800 men for the assault on July 3rd.'

entries.name: Thomas's Brigade, Pender's Division
entries.notes: Long Lane July 2-3 — 'engaged most of the day in severe skirmishing and exposed to heavy artillery fire' (tablet, in-window emitter).

entries.name: Scales's Brigade (Lowrance's), Pender's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Alfred M. Scales (w July 1); Col. William Lee J. Lowrance (w July 3)
entries.notes: Lost ~2/3 July 1 below Seminary Ridge; Trimble's second line July 3.

strengthFigures.note: Brigade had lost almost two-thirds July 1 (Wikipedia, proportional only).

entries.name: Lane's Sumter (Georgia) Artillery Battalion (Anderson's Division)
entries.notes: 17 guns; Anderson's front, Seminary Ridge; 1,082 rounds July 2-3 (tablet grade A).

entries.name: Garnett's Artillery Battalion (Heth's Division)
entries.notes: 15 guns; near McMillan Woods July 3 — tablet: 'in reserve, not actively engaged' vs secondaries crediting his rifles (flagged conflict, full-cast survey).

entries.name: Poague's Artillery Battalion (Pender's Division)
entries.notes: 16 guns; behind Pettigrew, Spangler/McMillan Woods July 3 — 10 guns engaged, 6 howitzers sheltered 'no active part' (documented internal split, tablet).

entries.name: McIntosh's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery, Col. R. Lindsay Walker)
entries.notes: 16 guns; Schultz Woods July 3; fired in the cannonade (sheet 8 names batteries; grade A).

entries.name: Danville (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: McIntosh's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery, Col. R. Lindsay Walker)

entries.name: Hardaway (Alabama) Artillery
parentChain: McIntosh's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery, Col. R. Lindsay Walker)
entries.notes: Two Whitworth rifles.

entries.name: 2nd Rockbridge (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: McIntosh's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery, Col. R. Lindsay Walker)

entries.name: Virginia (Johnson's) Battery
parentChain: McIntosh's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery, Col. R. Lindsay Walker)

entries.name: Pegram's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery)
entries.commander: Maj. William J. Pegram; Capt. Ervin B. Brunson
entries.notes: 20 guns; opened the battle July 1 (Marye's shot); Seminary Ridge S of Fairfield Rd July 3; fired in the cannonade (grade A).

entries.name: Crenshaw (Virginia) Battery
parentChain: Pegram's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery)
entries.commander: Capt. William G. Crenshaw (Wikipedia OOB) — some modern compilations give Lt. Andrew B. Johnston at Gettysburg; disagreement kept

entries.name: Fredericksburg (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Pegram's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery)
entries.notes: Fired the battle's first artillery shot, morning July 1 (attested tradition).

entries.name: Letcher (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Pegram's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Pee Dee (South Carolina) Artillery
parentChain: Pegram's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Purcell (Virginia) Artillery
parentChain: Pegram's Artillery Battalion (Third Corps reserve artillery)

entries.name: Hampton's Brigade, Stuart's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Wade Hampton (w July 3); Col. Laurence S. Baker
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived July 2 (Hunterstown fight); East Cavalry Field July 3 — off the terrain square.

entries.name: Fitzhugh Lee's Brigade, Stuart's Division
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived July 2 evening; East Cavalry Field July 3 — off the terrain square.

entries.name: W. H. F. Lee's Brigade (Chambliss's), Stuart's Division
entries.commander: Col. John R. Chambliss Jr. (Brig. Gen. W. H. F. Lee wounded at Brandy Station)
entries.arrivalNote: East Cavalry Field July 3 — off the terrain square.

entries.name: Jenkins's Brigade, Stuart's Division
entries.commander: Brig. Gen. Albert G. Jenkins (w July 2); Col. Milton J. Ferguson (Lt. Col. Vincent Witcher at East Cavalry Field)
entries.arrivalNote: Advanced with Ewell (mounted infantry role); East Cavalry Field July 3 — off the terrain square.

entries.name: Robertson's Brigade, Stuart's Division
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived July 3 (rear-guard duty); Fairfield corridor — off the terrain square.

entries.name: Jones's Brigade, Stuart's Division
entries.arrivalNote: Arrived July 3; fought the 6th US Cavalry at Fairfield (~13 km SW) — off the terrain square.

entries.name: Imboden's Command (attached cavalry brigade)
entries.arrivalNote: Reached the army's rear ~noon July 3; assigned the trains — Cashtown corridor, off the terrain square.

entries.name: Stuart Horse Artillery Battalion
entries.notes: Batteries split between East Cavalry Field (off-map), Fairfield, and the trains; Hart's section fought at South Cavalry Field July 3 (full-cast survey).

entries.name: Breathed's (Virginia) Battery, Stuart Horse Artillery
entries.notes: East Cavalry Field July 3 (off-map).

entries.name: Chew's (Virginia) Battery, Stuart Horse Artillery
entries.notes: With Jones's brigade (Fairfield corridor).

entries.name: Griffin's 2nd Baltimore (Maryland) Battery, Stuart Horse Artillery
Hart's Washington (South Carolina) Battery, Stuart Horse Artillery
entries.notes: A section engaged at South Cavalry Field vs Merritt July 3 (on-map edge; full-cast survey).

entries.name: McGregor's (Virginia) Battery, Stuart Horse Artillery
entries.notes: East Cavalry Field July 3 (off-map).

entries.name: McClanahan's (Virginia) Battery (Imboden's command)
entries.notes: With Imboden and the trains.

entries.name: XII Corps headquarters (corps command)
entries.commander: Maj. Gen. Henry W. Slocum (commanding the right wing July 1-3); Brig. Gen. Alpheus S. Williams (commanding the corps in that interval; Ruger the 1st Division)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 8 (the ED-29 evening chain's absent-then-returning frame): the CA-J2E-1 detachment order, the left-wing round trip (the Lockwood recapture, the quite-dark halt), the wrong-road discovery near midnight, and the July 3 daylight plan. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-xii-hq-slocum-williams.md.

entries.name: 2nd Division, XII Corps headquarters (division command)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 8: the division command record over Greene's stand — the 16:00 Latimer counter-duel sections, the 19:00 move-away order, the astray two-brigade night march (Williams's 'By some mistake'), Kane's fired-on return, the July 3 seven-hour defense. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-xii-2-hq-geary.md.

entries.name: Johnson's Division headquarters (division command)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 8 (register maintenance): the division command record of the Culp's Hill assault (the 16:00 Latimer order, the Walker detention discretion, the dark-at-Rock-Creek pin, the October 7 divisional casualty table) is carried on the four brigade dossiers; division tablet tab-csa-2c-joh-hq. No standalone dossier this pass.

entries.name: I Corps headquarters (corps command)
entries.commander: Maj. Gen. John F. Reynolds (also commanding the left wing; k July 1 ~10:15); Maj. Gen. Abner Doubleday (July 1); Maj. Gen. John Newton (July 2-3)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 10 (the ED-26 morning chain's command node): the Reynolds 10:15 death pin (tablet = Wadsworth = Doubleday, three-primary agreement), the wing-command succession ladder (Reynolds -> Doubleday corps / Howard field), and the corps' 8,200 -> ~2,450 July 1 arc. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-i-hq-reynolds.md.

entries.name: XI Corps headquarters (corps command)
entries.commander: Maj. Gen. Oliver O. Howard (commanding the field ~11:30 July 1 until Hancock/Slocum; corps to Schurz in that interval, resumed evening July 1)
entries.notes: Added dossier pass 10 (the ED-26/27 Howard-assumes-command sub-anchor + the CA-J1P-7 dual-command record): Howard's 11.30 = Schurz's 11.30 two-primary agreement; the 16:00/16:10/16:30 fall-back ladder; the Cemetery Hill rally frame. Dossier: reconstruction/dossiers/us-xi-hq-howard.md.
